title: New images 2026-04-24
body:
From https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/440

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests
-->

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [ ] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [ ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [ ] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [ ] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [ ] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
```
not ok 71 |030| podman run - idmapped mounts in 9066ms
         # tags: ci:parallel
         # (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/[helpers.bash, line 230](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d634aac4a16798c7447a909331d4dd20500daa0b/test/system/helpers.bash#L230),
         #  from function `die' in file test/system/[helpers.bash, line 967](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d634aac4a16798c7447a909331d4dd20500daa0b/test/system/helpers.bash#L967),
         #  from function `run_podman' in file test/system/[helpers.bash, line 608](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d634aac4a16798c7447a909331d4dd20500daa0b/test/system/helpers.bash#L608),
         #  in test file test/system/[030-run.bats, line 1523](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d634aac4a16798c7447a909331d4dd20500daa0b/test/system/030-run.bats#L1523))
```

https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6130573091012608/html/sys-podman-fedora-43-root-host.log.html


And this only in a few container tests, I assume because of cgroupfs fs driver and no systemd for cgroup management?

```
[FAILED] stop --all -t0 error logged
           Expected
               <string>: cannot delete path `/sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-8788856e524ff5bde9e6fa34942f30a4019bed1e70f02fc7201445ec5080b276`: Bad file descriptor
           to be empty
```


https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5145410672525312/html/int-podman-fedora-43-root-container.log.html#t--Podman-run-podman-run-shm-size-systemd--1


@giuseppe PTAL looks like this might be regressions in the new crun 1.27?

> @giuseppe PTAL looks like this might be regressions in the new crun 1.27?

yes it is, I am working on a fix right now

> > @giuseppe PTAL looks like this might be regressions in the new crun 1.27?
> 
> yes it is, I am working on a fix right now

https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/2069
Thank you, and what about the idmapping system test error? I assume the path addresses the cgroup cleanup only?
I have not managed yet to reproduce, I will need to debug it remotely

Ok I just gave it try a locally, a simple test run does indeed not reproduce with `hack/bats --root 030:"idmapped mounts"`

But these test run in parallel so I suspected that play a role and indeed running the full file in parallel with `hack/bats --root --tag=ci:parallel  030` failed with the same issue.

Looking at my hosts mounts we do leak mounts
```
─/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/my-volume-t61-r9apevla/_data/resolv.conf
│                                          tmpfs[/containers/storage/overlay-containers/274e6213fc4c45494d3ab6291fa27c3276c5c3b7dc3c4eb3c56d237f1968b3d2/userdata/resolv.conf//deleted]
│                                                                             tmpfs           rw,nosuid,nodev,seclabel,size=6520524k,nr_inodes=819200,mode=755,inode64
├─/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/my-volume-t61-r9apevla/_data/hosts
│                                          tmpfs[/containers/storage/overlay-containers/274e6213fc4c45494d3ab6291fa27c3276c5c3b7dc3c4eb3c56d237f1968b3d2/userdata/hosts//deleted]
│                                                                             tmpfs           rw,nosuid,nodev,seclabel,size=6520524k,nr_inodes=819200,mode=755,inode64
├─/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/my-volume-t61-r9apevla/_data/hostname
│                                          tmpfs[/containers/storage/overlay-containers/274e6213fc4c45494d3ab6291fa27c3276c5c3b7dc3c4eb3c56d237f1968b3d2/userdata/hostname//deleted]
│                                                                             tmpfs           rw,nosuid,nodev,seclabel,size=6520524k,nr_inodes=819200,mode=755,inode64
├─/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/my-volume-t61-aooeqchg/_data/hostname
│                                          tmpfs[/containers/storage/overlay-containers/8f528c5f32c831a42efc32723e399c836c84a30a9216ef84b24008581567e2ec/userdata/hostname//deleted]
│                                                                             tmpfs           rw,nosuid,nodev,seclabel,size=6520524k,nr_inodes=819200,mode=755,inode64
├─/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/my-volume-t61-aooeqchg/_data/resolv.conf
│                                          tmpfs[/containers/storage/overlay-containers/8f528c5f32c831a42efc32723e399c836c84a30a9216ef84b24008581567e2ec/userdata/resolv.conf//deleted]
│                                                                             tmpfs           rw,nosuid,nodev,seclabel,size=6520524k,nr_inodes=819200,mode=755,inode64
└─/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/my-volume-t61-aooeqchg/_data/hosts
                                           tmpfs[/containers/storage/overlay-containers/8f528c5f32c831a42efc32723e399c836c84a30a9216ef84b24008581567e2ec/userdata/hosts//deleted]
                                                                              tmpfs           rw,nosuid,nodev,seclabel,size=6520524k,nr_inodes=819200,mode=755,inode64

```

The /etc mount files get copied up into the volume because the test mounts `$myvolume:/etc:idmap`, I wonder if the copyup and idmap order of the bind mounts is not consistent and racy?
It failed 4 out of 4 times when using the parallel full file run so it fails consistently that way AFAICT

Ok so checking the volumes content the first run works as the volume is mepty and then copies up the content

```
   drwxr-xr-x. 1    0    0   614 Oct 11  2024 .
   drwx------. 1 1000 1000    10 Apr 16 20:37 ..
   -rw-r--r--. 1    0    0     7 Sep  6  2024 alpine-release
   drwxr-xr-x. 1    0    0    84 Oct 11  2024 apk
   drwxr-xr-x. 1    0    0    42 Oct 11  2024 busybox-paths.d
   drwxr-xr-x. 1    0    0     8 Sep  6  2024 crontabs
   -rw-r--r--. 1    0    0    89 May  6  2024 fstab
   -rw-r--r--. 1    0    0   510 May  6  2024 group
   -rw-r--r--. 0 1000 1000    13 Apr 16 20:37 hostname
   -rw-r--r--. 0 1000 1000   279 Apr 16 20:37 hosts
```

The uid of our special files are 1000 and hence then not mapped in the second run userns and hence crun fails to mount over it?

If I run without the parallel all files are owned by uid 0 as expected.

Consider the idmap mounts leak is I guess it could be the same as https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28507? Though not sure how the paralle vs non parallel test run influences that.
crun compiled from main 6f0a3c5013e62c25f6eaa6e049aabc8b504d4e66 does NOT fix the issue locally for me so maybe not the same issue

thanks, that was helpful.

I've added a revert commit to https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/2067 to fix it

I'll cut a new release as soon as https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/2067 and https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/2069 are merged
@giuseppe thanks for the fixes! looks like they merged, let me know once a release has been cut
tmt tests failed for commit 26ac99e5914c52da9b81d3b8167ef85be08cbe13. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.
tmt tests failed for commit 1b99ae56b0e1b3ec48855fa26754c15e2e33ee7e. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.

Yep, good for review, but the test failure seems consistent with https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28570/checks?check_run_id=7298214609 so it might be a real failure? I'll start it one more time

> Yep, good for review, but the test failure seems consistent with https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28570/checks?check_run_id=7298214609 so it might be a real failure? I'll start it one more time

Tests are passed now. I have seen the failure before so it is a flake and not new from this CI image.
